posts.author: Charanjeev_Kohli
posts.text: Thanks Simple back of the envolepe calculkations, Management shall be selling around Right REs 15 Cr of shares, this will create lot of liquidity in short span, to avoid that they shall go to Bulk buyers (family offices & all that), at Rs 10 premium, management shall get Rs 150 Cr., as per NCLT ruling they infused Rs 205 Cr in the company. So virtually management is getting 1500* cr of market cap free of cost in 2 years (currtent m cap of 1800 Cr + 200 cr rights / 75% post right holding). posts.author: Amit_Agarwal1
posts.text: Yes Mr. Kohli it seems like a good special situation. The track record of Eagle group in executing such projects have to be examined. Although one should not invest in a rights issue where management is not participating but this situation is different. The promoters have to bring their stake down to comply with SEBI norms. Also this company has been recently acquired by the new promoters,thier acquisition cost and value added by them over last 3 years must be analysed otherwise it would become a case of mopping their initial investment at the cost of new investors.
